issues: No dedicated canonical node for rational functions; mapping to reciprocal graphs and asymptotes under-represents sketching general rational functions, oblique asymptotes and set-of-values-by-discriminant work.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a dedicated rational-functions node or retain mapping with a coverage caveat.

issues: Catalog lacks nodes for y^2=f(x), y=1/f(x) and y=f(|x|); only modulus graphs and generic transformation nodes are available.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create specific graph-relation nodes or accept this as a partial mapping.

issues: Matrix subtraction is not represented by a separate canonical node; only addition, multiplication and zero/identity nodes are listed.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping and ensure subtraction is covered alongside addition.

issues: No dedicated node for inverse transformation matrices; mapped to generic 2D transformations and inverse matrices.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add an inverse-transformation node or retain with caveat.

issues: Area scale factor of a transformation lacks a dedicated canonical node; only determinant and 2D transformation nodes are used.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add an area-scale-factor node or retain mapping.

issues: Angles between lines/planes are not represented by dedicated angle nodes but are subsumed by scalar product and line/plane intersection nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep as mapped; no action required unless finer granularity is needed.

issues: Proof by induction has no dedicated canonical node; mapping to proof by deduction and logical argument is a coarse approximation of a required assessment technique.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create a proof-by-induction node under Proof Methods.

issues: No dedicated canonical node for systems of three linear simultaneous equations, their consistency or geometric interpretation; only singularity/inverse and eigenvalue nodes are used.
Expectation of a function of X, the PDF-CDF relationship and CDFs of related variables are not represented by dedicated canonical nodes; only PDF, mean and probability nodes are used.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add CRV transformation/expectation and CDF nodes.

issues: Pooled estimate of population variance and normal-distribution tests for difference of means lack dedicated canonical nodes; only t-test and t-confidence-interval nodes are used.
Fitting a theoretical distribution to data is subsumed by the chi-squared goodness-of-fit node but has no separate representation.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep as mapped or add a distribution-fitting node.

issues: Normal approximations for the sign and Wilcoxon tests are not represented by dedicated canonical nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add normal-approximation nodes for non-parametric tests.

overallIssues.issue: The canonical catalog lacks several Further Pure Mathematics 1 nodes, leading to weak or acceptable mappings: rational functions and oblique asymptotes (1.2-RATIONALS), graph relations y^2=f(x), y=1/f(x) and y=f(|x|) (1.2-GRAPHRELS), inverse transformation matrices (1.4-TRANINVERSE), area scale factor (1.4-AREASCALE), and proof by induction (1.7).

Further Pure Mathematics 2 matrices content maps to eigenvalue and Cayley-Hamilton nodes, but the canonical catalog has no node for 3x3 linear simultaneous equations, consistency cases or geometric interpretation.

Further Probability & Statistics points are under-represented in the catalog: continuous random variable expectations of functions, CDF/PDF relationships and transformations of related variables (4.1); pooled variance and normal-distribution difference-of-means tests (4.2); and normal approximations for non-parametric tests (4.4).
Fitting a theoretical distribution prior to a chi-squared goodness-of-fit test (4.3) is not separately represented, though it is subsumed by the chi-squared goodness-of-fit node.

Paper 4 (Further Probability & Statistics) sections 4.1–4.4 are only partially mapped and no explanatory reasons are provided; distinct official bullets such as CDFs of related variables, pooled variance estimates, normal-distribution tests for a difference of means, confidence intervals for a difference of means, and fitting theoretical distributions are not represented by dedicated canonical nodes.

Several Paper 1 sub-points are mapped only partially or through overly broad canonical nodes, leaving gaps for oblique asymptotes and set-of-values via discriminant in rational functions, the relationship between a transformation and its inverse, area-scale-factor links, and proof by induction (mapped only to generic deductive-proof nodes).

Decomposition granularity is inconsistent: sections 1.1–1.4 and 3.1–3.4 are split into individual bullets, whereas 1.5–1.7, 2.1–2.6, 3.5–3.6 and all Paper 4 sections are represented by single broad official points, making precise coverage verification harder.
One mapping uses a prior-knowledge canonical node rather than a 9231-specific node: linear-momentum conservation in 3.6 is linked to MECH-MOMI-LINM-CONS (assumed from 9709).

The candidate index is limited to subject-content syllabus points; non-content sections of the official specification (aims, assessment objectives, command words, prior-knowledge requirements and MF19 formula/table sections) are not represented.
